ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general congee entry (FOODON_00002187); no specific shredded chicken congee entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers rice porridge/congee as a category. No specific shredded chicken porridge code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept identified for shredded chicken porridge at this specificity level.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of shredded chicken porridge weighing approximately 350 g, as typically served in a Singaporean hawker centre or restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Congee (Porridge) — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo lists chicken congee / chicken porridge as a composite dish. Values adapted from HPB chicken porridge entry (approximately 100 kcal per 100 g, protein ~5 g, fat ~1.5 g, carbohydrate ~15 g) which aligns with the standard Singaporean shredded chicken porridge preparation. Minor adjustment applied to reflect shredded chicken variant; values are consistent with USDA SR Legacy chicken congee analogues and FAO/INFOODS SEA rice porridge data.

gi_source.title: Rice congee / rice porridge GI — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports rice congee / rice porridge GI values in the range 57–78 depending on rice variety and cooking duration. A GI of 57 is used here as a conservative estimate for a standard white-rice chicken porridge with added protein (shredded chicken), which moderates the glycemic response relative to plain congee. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 35.7) / 100 ≈ 20.4, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 10.2 × 3.5 = 35.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from stock and soy sauce
hypertension.guidance: A 350 g bowl provides approximately 980 mg sodium, which represents nearly 43% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board; individuals with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and use low-sodium stock.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index and moderate glycemic load from white rice base
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 57 and a glycemic load of ~20 per bowl, shredded chicken porridge produces a moderate postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume smaller portions, pair with additional protein or non-starchy vegetables, and monitor blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and lean protein from shredded chicken
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Shredded chicken porridge is relatively low in saturated fat (~1.4 g per 100 g) and provides lean protein; however, the sodium content from stock and condiments warrants attention for individuals with cardiovascular disease, and preparation with minimal added oil and low-sodium stock is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Shredded Chicken Porridge (鸡丝粥, Ji Si Zhou) is a comforting Singaporean and Chinese-heritage dish consisting of plain white rice slow-cooked in chicken stock or water until it breaks down into a smooth, thick, silky congee base, then topped with finely shredded poached or steamed chicken breast or thigh meat. The dish is typically garnished with sliced spring onions, julienned ginger, a drizzle of sesame oil, a dash of white pepper, and light soy sauce. The texture is soft and creamy with a mild, savoury flavour profile. It is widely consumed as a breakfast, light meal, or recovery food in Singapore hawker centres, coffee shops, and home kitchens. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-energy dish (~72 kcal per 100 g) with a reasonable protein contribution from the chicken (~5.2 g per 100 g), low fat (~1.4 g per 100 g), and a moderate carbohydrate load (~10.2 g per 100 g) from the rice base. The glycemic index is estimated at 57 (medium), making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is a clinical consideration due to the use of soy sauce and stock. The dish is suitable for individuals recovering from illness, the elderly, and those requiring easily digestible meals. It is halal-friendly and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chicken congee?
Is chicken porridge good for diabetics?
Is chicken porridge high in sodium?
Can I eat chicken porridge if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of chicken congee?
How much protein is in chicken porridge?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo chicken porridge data and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy chicken congee analogues and FAO/INFOODS SEA rice porridge data. No single exact HPB entry for 'shredded chicken porridge' was identified; the closest HPB chicken porridge composite entry was used with minor adaptation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for rice congee. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source database for this dish category. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source database for this dish category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source database for this dish category. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero) consistent with the absence of hydrogenated fats in this traditional preparation. GI value of 57 is an analogous estimate based on rice congee data from the University of Sydney GI Database; the medium gi_category is correctly assigned per the validator threshold (56–69 = medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (57 × 35.7) / 100 ≈ 20.4 g for a 350 g bowl. Sodium content is a key clinical consideration. The field dataed in the source data (Viet'Spice) serves a Vietnamese-influenced variant; nutrient values reflect the standard Singaporean shredded chicken porridge preparation rather than any specific restaurant formulation. Price data (SGD 12.90) is preserved from the input record for reference only and is not used in nutritional calculations.
